other_observers: Nick Komar, Nick A. Komar, and Dan Lazlo; Nick Komar originally found the bird. There were alot of others that saw the bird including, most of the people that came back from the CFO convention.
specific_location: When I saw the bird it was on the south side of Duck Lake. Duck Lake is about 2 or 3 miles west from the Windsor Exit.
description: It had a dark rufous to purplish belly and breast. Gray head and neck. Top of the head was dark. It had a white supercillium. Bill slightly upturned, orange with a black tip, and slightly shorter than a Marbled Godwit. The back was black with white spotting. Priamries and secondaries were gray. The undertail covert was mostly white with some black barring. Black legs. The size was slightly smaller than a Green-winged Teal. Mostly black tail.Behaviors: It was feeding in water that was about 6 inches deep.Call: Didn't hear it.Plumage: Breeding Adult
